steps.title: Identify all solutions from the factored form
steps.content:
The equation $x^2(4x - 13)(4x - u) = 0$ is already factored. Setting each factor equal to zero:
  • From $x^2 = 0$: $x = 0$ (multiplicity 2)
  • From $4x - 13 = 0$: $x = \frac{13}{4}$
  • From $4x - u = 0$: $x = \frac{u}{4}$

steps.title: Set up the sum equation with multiplicity
steps.content:
The sum of solutions (counting each root by its multiplicity) equals 25:
$$0 + 0 + \frac{13}{4} + \frac{u}{4} = 25$$
Combine the fractions:
$$\frac{13 + u}{4} = 25$$

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 4:
$$13 + u = 100$$
Subtract 13 from both sides:
$$u = 87$$
